author: dojo-community
name: transform
context: Rewrite JSON objects and arrays with assignments, paths, entries, and reducers instead of hand-editing files
info: This skill covers the jq features used when agents need to patch config files, normalize API payloads, redact keys, or restructure data before persisting it. It focuses on update assignment (`|=`), arithmetic assignment, plain assignment, `setpath`, `getpath`, `delpaths`, `to_entries` and `from_entries`, `with_entries`, and reducers that build new JSON values. The bundled script supports safe temp-file rewrites so an agent can patch `package.json`, deployment manifests, or generated artifacts without relying on brittle text substitution.
triggers: rewrite a json file with jq
update a field in package json
set nested json path with jq
transform api payload into a new object
body:
# jq Transform

Load this skill when jq should produce a modified JSON document, not just a filtered view.

## Assignment Operators

The official manual distinguishes plain assignment `=` from update assignment `|=`. Use `|=` when the right-hand side should run against the existing value at the path, and use `=` when the right-hand side should run against the whole input.

~~~bash
jq ".version = \"1.2.4\"" package.json
jq ".count += 1" counters.json
jq "(.features[] | select(.name == \"beta\") | .enabled) |= true" config.json
~~~

## Path Helpers

Use `setpath`, `getpath`, and `delpaths` when the target key path is easier to describe as data than as a hard-coded expression.

~~~bash
jq "setpath([\"deploy\", \"region\"]; \"us-east-1\")" app.json
jq "getpath([\"metadata\", \"owner\"])" app.json
jq "delpaths([[ \"debug\" ], [ \"metadata\", \"trace\" ]])" app.json
~~~

## Reshaping Objects

Object-entry helpers are the reliable way to rename keys, map values, or turn arrays into index objects.

~~~bash
jq "with_entries(.key |= ascii_downcase)" env.json
jq "to_entries | map(select(.value != null)) | from_entries" payload.json
jq "group_by(.environment) | map({key: .[0].environment, value: length}) | from_entries" deploys.json
~~~

## Safe File Rewrite Pattern

jq does not edit files in place. Write to a temp file, verify success, then move the temp file over the original.

~~~bash
tmp=$(mktemp)
jq -S ".scripts.test = \"vitest\"" package.json > "$tmp" && mv "$tmp" package.json
git diff -- package.json
~~~

For reusable filters in program files, continue to [[jq/modules]].

sections.title: Assignment operators
sections.body: Use `|=` when the update depends on the existing value at the selected path, such as `.count += 1` or `.name |= ascii_downcase`. Use plain `=` when the right-hand side should evaluate against the whole input document. The difference matters when copying values across the document or emitting multiple results.

sections.title: Path-oriented updates
sections.body: `setpath`, `getpath`, and `delpaths` are the path-data equivalents of hard-coded field expressions. They are especially useful when the path comes from another tool or needs to be generated inside a jq reduction.

sections.title: Reshaping and normalization
sections.body: Use `to_entries`, `from_entries`, and `with_entries` to rename keys, filter nulls, or transform object values. Use `reduce` to build index objects or summaries from arrays. This is the main pattern for normalizing upstream JSON before saving it or handing it to another tool.

sections.title: Safe file rewrites
sections.body: jq itself does not have an in-place flag. The safe pattern is temp file plus atomic move. Sort keys with `-S` when stable diffs help review, and always inspect the result with [[git/diff]] or another comparison tool before committing large generated changes.

scripts.name: Transform JSON
scripts.description: Run a jq transform and optionally replace a target file through a temp-file rewrite

JQ_INPUT.description: Inline JSON or raw text input

JQ_NULL_INPUT.description: Use -n and do not read a file or stdin

JQ_RAW_INPUT.description: Treat input as raw text via -R

JQ_SLURP.description: Read all inputs into one array or one raw string via -s

JQ_INPLACE.description: Replace JQ_FILE via temp file after a successful transform

JQ_BACKUP_SUFFIX.description: Optional suffix for a backup copy before overwrite, such as .bak

JQ_VARS_JSON.description: JSON object whose keys become jq variables through --argjson

input.description: Inline JSON or raw text input

backupSuffix.description: Optional suffix for a pre-overwrite backup copy

stdout.description: Transformed JSON when not rewriting a file

file.description: Target file path when inPlace is true

exitCode.description: Process exit code from jq or the wrapper script

depends.reason: Transform commands rely on correct flag selection and variable handling
The packaged script runs in Bash
Temp-file rewrite patterns are required for safe file replacement
